name: apple
healthBenefits: Good source of vitamin C and fiber! Help with digestion and overall metabolic health.

name: apricot
healthBenefits: They contain beta-carotene and vitamin A, supporting eye health. Rich in antioxidants.

name: avocado
healthBenefits: Source of monounsaturated fats helps support heart healty and vitamin E to support immune function.

name: bananas
healthBenefits: Great source of potassium for blood pressure support. According to USDA, they also contain vitamin B6 and fiber.

name: blueberries
healthBenefits: High in antioxidant, may help reduce oxidative stress in cells.

name: cherries
healthBenefits: Low callories and provide vitamin C and anthocyanins, linked to reduced inflammation.

name: clementine
healthBenefits: High in vitamin C that support immune function and collagen formation.

name: coconut
healthBenefits: Dietary fiber and essential minerals like manganese, provide a lot of energy with fatty meats.

name: custardapple
healthBenefits: Also known as cherimoya contain vitamin C and dietary fiber, aiding digestion. Very high caloric.

name: dragonfruit
healthBenefits: Vit C and unique antioxidants like betacyanins. Healthy and happy gut!

name: feijoa
healthBenefits: Rich in vitamin C and phenolic compounds.

name: fig
healthBenefits: Figs contain dietary fiber, which may aid digestion, and small amounts of calcium.

name: granadilla
healthBenefits: Granadilla (a type of passion fruit) is high in vit C.

name: grape
healthBenefits: Grapes are known for their resveratrol antioxidant, supports heart health, alhtough very high sugar content

name: guava
healthBenefits: Pretty high in vitamin C, more so than many citrus fruits.

name: kiwi
healthBenefits: Kiwis are really hight in  vitamin C and vitamin K.

name: lemon
healthBenefits: Provide vitamin C, great for skin and immune health. High in antioxidants, can reduce inflamation

name: lychee
healthBenefits: Lychees are rich in vitamin C and unique polyphenols.

name: mandarin
healthBenefits: Offer vitamin C and a sweet citrus flavor. Also low caloric.

name: mango
healthBenefits: Mangos contain beta-carotene, supporting eye health.

name: mangosteen
healthBenefits: Mangosteens contain xanthones, powerful antioxidants.

name: melon
healthBenefits: Melons like cantaloupe are high in vitamin A and C. According to USDA, they can help maintain healthy vision and tissue growth.

name: orange
healthBenefits: Oranges are a great source of vitamin C, crucial for collagen synthesis.

name: papaya
healthBenefits: Papayas are recognized for their papain enzyme and vitamin C. USDA data adds that they may aid digestion and reduce inflammation.

name: passionfruit
healthBenefits: Passion fruit is known for its tangy flavor and high vitamin C. NIH suggests its seeds offer beneficial fiber and protein.

name: peach
healthBenefits: Provide vitamin C, plus beta-carotene. According to USDA, they can support healthy skin and metabolism.

name: pear
healthBenefits: Offer soluble fiber, that helps to regulate digestion. NIH indicates their vitamin C content may bolster immune defenses.

name: persimmon
healthBenefits: Contain beta-carotene, supporting eye health. Also may protect cell integrity according to USDA references show their antioxidants .

name: pineapple
healthBenefits: Pineapple provides the enzyme bromelain, aiding digestion. Its C and manganese can also support bone health.

name: pomegranate
healthBenefits: Pomegranates are famed for their polyphenols. They may help lower inflammation and oxidative stress.

name: sapodilla
healthBenefits: Sapodilla is a sweet fruit containing vitamin C and fiber.

name: soursop
healthBenefits: Soursop has a tangy flavor and vitamin C content.

name: starfruit
healthBenefits: Provides vitamin C in a, moderating intake advised if you have kidney issues.

name: strawberry
healthBenefits: Strawberries deliver a strong vitamin C punch, aiding tissue repair. Also contain manganese for enzyme function.

name: tomato
healthBenefits: Tomatoes are famous for lycopene, which may support heart health.

name: watermelon
healthBenefits: Watermelon contains lycopene and beta-carotene, supporting skin and vision, and great hydrating properties in hot climates.
